categories.summary: The discussion channel covered several key topics. A critical issue emerged regarding the ai16z token migration, with odilitime confirming the migration period has closed, leaving nelsonlopes_ unable to migrate approximately $700-900 worth of tokens. A scam incident occurred where nelsonlopes_ lost funds through a fraudulent support ticket, with community members advising to block and report the scammers. On the technical side, cacophonousstrife sought guidance on building examples from the ElizaOS documentation, with odilitime directing them to the GitHub examples directory at github.com/elizaOS/eliza/tree/v2.0.0/examples. The community discussed ElizaOS v3 development, with satsbased announcing it's nearly ready and will enable agents to generate revenue. A significant clarification was provided about the ElizaOK product: baogerbao explained that ElizaOK is built with elizacloud, has no separate token currently, and all users registered under elizaok flow into elizacloud. Questions arose about the relationship between ElizaOS, ElizaOK, and elizacloud, with baogerbao deferring detailed business model explanations to odilitime. The token deployment was clarified as being on Solana, BSC, and Base chains. Technical support was requested for minimax token plan key integration as a provider plugin, though this remained unresolved. Community engagement initiatives were emphasized, with calls to interact with Shaw's posts and share development updates.

faqs.question: How can I do the migration for ai16z tokens in my ledger?
Does anyone know how to build the examples on docs.elizaos.ai/what-you-can-build or have a tutorial?
Does ElizaOK have a separate token or does everything connect with $ELIZAOS?
faqs.answeredBy: baogerbao - elizaOK has no token for now, built with elizacloud

faqs.question: What's the utilities of the ElizaOS token within the ecosystem and how does ElizaOS connect to ElizaOK?
faqs.answeredBy: baogerbao - deferred to odilitime for detailed reply

faqs.question: Can AI agents solve serious hacking problems via enhancing security keys in DEFI space?
Any tips for making minimax token plan key the provider for eliza?
Is elizaos back on sol? Which ticker is it?
faqs.answeredBy: satsbased - on sol, bsc and base

helpInteractions.context: Needed guidance on building examples from ElizaOS documentation
helpInteractions.resolution: Provided GitHub link to examples directory at github.com/elizaOS/eliza/tree/v2.0.0/examples

helpInteractions.context: Lost $700-900 to scam through fraudulent support ticket
helpInteractions.resolution: Advised to block and report the scammers

helpInteractions.context: Confusion about ElizaOK token and relationship to ElizaOS ecosystem
helpInteractions.resolution: Clarified elizaOK has no token, built with elizacloud, users flow to elizacloud

helpInteractions.context: Confusion about which blockchain ElizaOS token is deployed on
helpInteractions.resolution: Clarified token is on Solana, BSC, and Base chains

actionItems.description: Complete ElizaOS v3 development - nearly ready, will enable agents to generate revenue
Resolve minimax token plan key integration as provider plugin
Provide clearer documentation on ElizaOS token utilities and ecosystem connections between ElizaOS, ElizaOK, and elizacloud
Explore AI agent solutions for enhancing DEFI security against hacking

categories.summary: igor.peregudov announced the release of @elisym/plugin-elizaos, a plugin that integrates ElizaOS v1 agents with the Elisym decentralized AI-agent marketplace. The plugin enables agents to become paid providers by publishing capability cards over Nostr using NIP-89, accepting encrypted job requests via NIP-90, executing them through the agent's model or SKILL.md tool-use loop, and collecting SOL payments on Solana. The implementation includes 110 tests with CI on every PR and is signed with GitHub Actions provenance. A PR to the registry (#346) is open for review. The plugin is available on npm and GitHub. stan0473 requested a naming convention change to either plugin-elisym or plugin-elizaos-elisym, which igor.peregudov promptly implemented. kishark0673 posted seeking development opportunities.

faqs.question: Could you please rename the plugin into plugin-elisym or plugin-elizaos-elisym?

helpInteractions.context: Plugin naming convention needed to follow project standards
helpInteractions.resolution: igor.peregudov renamed the plugin to follow the requested naming convention

actionItems.description: Review PR #346 for @elisym/plugin-elizaos-elisym in the registry
Update plugin documentation to reflect new name plugin-elizaos-elisym

categories.summary: The channel discussion centered around a lawsuit mentioned via a Twitter link, with community members reacting to legal action against the project. The key technical discussion involved stan0473 mentioning plans to build a plugin for scammers, with odilitime offering to share a plugin he had already started working on. The main substantive exchange occurred when hide.o.n asked about the team's response to the lawsuit. Odilitime explained the team would respond through their lawyers, emphasizing they have code to prove they built everything promised. He characterized the lawsuit as without merit and made specific reference to 'number 3' of the lawsuit. The discussion also included multiple scam warnings from community members (chulylooly and gwen_217) targeting suspicious users. Several users joined asking general questions about tickets and crypto/AI learning, but these received no substantive responses. The overall tone suggested the team is confident in their legal position based on their development work and code documentation.

faqs.question: Is there a ticket here?
How does the team plan to respond to this matter?

helpInteractions.context: stan0473 mentioned building a plugin for scammers
helpInteractions.resolution: odilitime offered to send the plugin he had already started working on

actionItems.description: Build a plugin for scammers

categories.summary: The Discord chat segment for the #💬-coders channel contains minimal technical discussion. The primary focus is on a CI/CD workflow issue affecting a pull request. igor.peregudov reported that the CI pipeline is failing on PR #346 in the elizaos-plugins/registry repository due to a configuration problem with the claude-code-action workflow. The specific issue is that the workflow cannot fetch an OIDC token because of missing permissions - either the `id-token: write` permission or `github_token` is not properly configured. This is identified as a repository configuration issue rather than a problem with the code changes in the PR itself. igor.peregudov requested that someone with appropriate permissions fix the workflow configuration so the PR can proceed to review. odilitime responded asking for clarification about which repository was affected, and stan0473 provided the direct link to the problematic PR. Additionally, satsbased made a brief comment encouraging open source contributions, though this was not connected to the main technical discussion thread.

faqs.question: Which repo has the CI failure?

helpInteractions.context: Needed to identify the specific repository with CI workflow issues
helpInteractions.resolution: Provided direct link to the PR (https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/346)

actionItems.description: Fix CI workflow configuration by adding `id-token: write` permission or `github_token` to resolve OIDC token fetch failure in claude-code-action for PR #346

categories.summary: The discussion centered on three main issues: communication challenges, token migration problems, and project viability concerns. Community members expressed frustration about the lack of official statements regarding an ongoing lawsuit, with hide.o.n requesting proactive communication from the team to prevent potential exchange delistings. odilitime explained limited access to official accounts and suggested legal counsel advised silence. The ElizaOS X account inactivity (nearly a month without posts) was raised as a concern for community engagement, though alexeill noted lawsuits typically reduce company communications. A significant token migration issue emerged when aeropedro discovered their self-custodied AI16Z tokens couldn't be migrated to ELIZAOS, as the migration window had closed. odilitime offered to add them to a waitlist but acknowledged it was a longshot. Technical discussion included vslappyx asking about v2.x/v3 stability for agent development, with stan0473 confirming it was ready and explaining the versioning convention (0.x=first, 1.x=second, 2.x=third version). Discord experienced technical issues with posts being auto-deleted without appearing in audit logs, affecting multiple users including flykite and nusko_. odilitime couldn't identify the cause but manually reposted content. nusko_ shared experience building a Jinx AI agent using ElizaOS that achieved 9M impressions and 3.3K followers before Twitter bans. Community sentiment was mixed, with lordwallet accusing the team of fraud and token dumping, while odilitime defended Shaw and noted continued GitHub activity. The discussion touched on project recovery prospects, with odilitime citing openclaw and hermes as proof of market demand, suggesting the token narrative damaged the project more than the technology itself.

faqs.question: Is v2.x/v3 stable enough to start exploring and building Eliza agents?
Why is the versioning/tagging convention confusing - why not tag v3 as v3?
How can I migrate my self-custodied AI16Z tokens to ELIZAOS?
Are my AI16Z tokens now useless since migration closed?
Who has access to post on the ElizaOS X account?
Why hasn't the ElizaOS X account posted in almost a month?
Are you people done with the project due to the lawsuit?

helpInteractions.context: User discovered token migration window closed for self-custodied AI16Z tokens
helpInteractions.resolution: Offered to add wallet to waitlist if migration reopens, though acknowledged it's a longshot

helpInteractions.context: User confused about version stability and naming convention for Eliza agents
helpInteractions.resolution: Confirmed v2.x is stable to work on and explained versioning system (0.x=v1, 1.x=v2, 2.x=v3)

helpInteractions.context: User's posts being auto-deleted by Discord without explanation
helpInteractions.resolution: Investigated audit logs, couldn't find cause, manually forwarded content from another channel

helpInteractions.context: User's posts auto-deleting without showing in logs, couldn't share app link
helpInteractions.resolution: Manually posted content via DM and shared to another developer server for visibility

actionItems.description: Post official statement about lawsuit on ElizaOS X account to address community concerns
Increase activity on ElizaOS X account with posts and retweets for community engagement
Investigate and fix Discord auto-moderation issue causing posts to be deleted without audit log entries
Consider reopening token migration window for self-custodied AI16Z holders
Improve project monetization strategy following openclaw and hermes market validation

categories.summary: The channel focused on three main technical developments: (1) ElizaOS v2/v3 migration planning - odilitime merged elisym into the elizaOS ecosystem, with stan0473 identifying the next quest as porting to v3. igor.peregudov confirmed v2 porting feasibility and inquired about stable release timing for v2 (currently in alpha). (2) Security incident - igor.peregudov lost 100k ai16z tokens to a wallet drainer at bulkdao.co/allocation, serving as a community warning about phishing attacks. The transaction was documented on Solscan. (3) LemonCake plugin launch - lemoncake03027 introduced an open-source (MIT) payment solution for autonomous agents to handle API paywalls. The plugin provides spend-capped, time-boxed, revocable Pay Tokens using USDC/JPYC on Polygon. Integration requires one line in ElizaOS v2 (eliza-plugin-lemoncake). Features include 32 paid APIs, auto-idempotency keys, structured receipts, QuickBooks/Xero/Zoho/freee integration, and KYA tier system. Also available for MCP (Claude/Cursor/Cline) and Dify Marketplace. Sandbox mode available for testing. odilitime approved the implementation.

faqs.question: Any thoughts on when we might see a stable release for v2?
How much was stolen from your wallet?
What paid APIs do you wish your agent could call?

helpInteractions.context: Wallet drainer phishing attack at bulkdao.co/allocation
helpInteractions.resolution: Warned community about the scam link and reverted edit to keep the URL visible for future reference

helpInteractions.context: Suggested keeping the scam link visible for community awareness
helpInteractions.resolution: igor.peregudov reverted the edit to maintain the warning

actionItems.description: Port elisym to ElizaOS v3
Release stable version of ElizaOS v2 (currently in alpha)
Add top-requested paid APIs to LemonCake marketplace
Onboard Eliza v2 builders with LemonCake sandbox credits

categories.summary: The discussion centered on community reorganization following Eliza Labs' transition. A new Cron Job podcast episode (S1E12) was released covering recent project developments including the shutdown announcement, community response, and continued code shipping. The key technical discussion involved clarification about project governance: odilitime confirmed the foundation remains funded and responsible for the token, contrary to assumptions that it was completely dissolved. quanteliza proposed creating a structured forum for community leadership to manage business aspects like social media and website, requesting transparency and organization. odilitime responded by offering access to an existing steering group focused on organizing marketing efforts and potential governance. valleybeyond7991 expressed interest in contributing to social media management and joined the steering group. The conversation also referenced external content including a fixupx post and an AI agent building contest. Overall, the segment shows the community actively working to establish clear organizational structure and leadership roles in the post-Eliza Labs era.

faqs.question: Is Eliza Labs gone and is it now just an open community with support?
What is steering?

helpInteractions.context: Confusion about whether Eliza Labs was completely dissolved and need for structured community organization
helpInteractions.resolution: Clarified that the foundation is still funded and responsible for the token, offered invitation to existing steering group

helpInteractions.context: Understanding what the steering group does and how to participate
helpInteractions.resolution: Explained steering group focuses on organizing marketing efforts and potentially governance, invited them to join

actionItems.description: Create new community forum section for discussing leadership structure, business operations, Twitter/website management with transparency
Establish structured and transparent governance for community leadership roles

categories.summary: The channel discussion covered two main technical topics. First, arkchain raised concerns about USDC freezing risks for cross-border agent operations and sought alternative payment solutions for agents operating in jurisdictions where asset freezing is a concern. Lemoncake03027 responded by promoting their payment infrastructure built specifically for agent machine-to-machine (M2M) payments, featuring spend-capped JWTs and kill switch functionality, and offered to create an Eliza plugin integration if needed. The second part of the conversation consisted of itssowenn4462 introducing themselves as an AI/ML and Full Stack Engineer, detailing their expertise in autonomous multi-agent systems, RAG pipelines with vector databases like FAISS and Pinecone, voice AI integrations using Vapi and Twilio, workflow automation platforms, and computer vision applications. Their tech stack spans React/Next.js/TypeScript on frontend, Node.js/Python with FastAPI and Django on backend, PostgreSQL/MongoDB/Redis for databases, OpenAI/LangChain/Transformers for AI/LLM work, and AWS infrastructure with Docker and CI/CD pipelines.

faqs.question: Are there alternatives to USDC for agents running cross-border or in contexts where USDC freezing is a concern?

helpInteractions.context: Looking for USDC alternatives for cross-border agent payments due to freezing concerns
helpInteractions.resolution: Offered payment infrastructure with spend-capped JWTs and kill switch, specifically built for agent M2M payments, with potential Eliza plugin integration

actionItems.description: Develop Eliza plugin integration for agent M2M payment rail with spend-capped JWTs and kill switch

categories.summary:
The main technical discussion centered around igor.peregudov's demonstration of the @elisym/plugin-elizaos-elisym plugin, which transforms Eliza agents into paid service providers. The plugin enables agent-to-agent transactions using a Nostr-based discovery layer for capability announcement and Solana for payment settlement. The demo showed a complete flow: an Eliza agent publishes its capabilities to Nostr, receives a paid request from Claude desktop for trending GitHub repos, settles payment on Solana devnet, and delivers the service. This creates a decentralized marketplace where agents can monetize their capabilities without intermediary platforms.

A significant discussion emerged around ELIZAOS token utility. quanteliza proposed a v3 feature allowing protocol fees to be paid in either USDC or ELIZAOS tokens, with discounts for token payments and automatic buyback/burn mechanisms for USDC payments, similar to early BNB tokenomics. This would create economic demand while maintaining the open-source framework. odilitime responded positively, stating this motivated him to complete his November work on enabling plugins to accept $elizaOS and $DegenAI as x402 payment, identifying this as the lowest-hanging fruit for token utility implementation.

Odilitime also announced updates to working groups after discussions with Shaw, and made steering bumps to key community members. There was brief discussion about Discord role management and layout feedback requests, though no specific feedback was provided in this segment.

faqs.question: Is there any way that the utility of the elisaos coin is connected to elisaos infrastructure?
What if v3 let you pay protocol fees in USDC or ELIZAOS, with a discount for paying in token + backend buyback/burn on USDC payments?

helpInteractions.context: Demonstrating how to implement and use the elisym plugin for monetizing Eliza agents
helpInteractions.resolution: Provided complete demo video, code repository, and example agent showing full agent-to-agent payment flow using Nostr discovery and Solana settlement

actionItems.description: Complete November work on enabling plugins to accept $elizaOS and $DegenAI as x402 payment
Update working groups following discussion with Shaw
Implement v3 protocol fees payable in USDC or ELIZAOS with token payment discounts and USDC buyback/burn mechanism
Configure auto-select functionality for 'I want to help' Discord role

categories.summary: The discussion centered on Hierarchical Task Networks (HTN) implementation in ElizaOS. thirti.eth inquired about building with alpha code and shared Claude's suggestion about upgrading HTN-lite to full HTN when Eliza v2 reaches beta, replacing hand-coded decomposition with LLM planning while maintaining existing triggers and steps. odilitime clarified HTN as a multistep approach. thirti.eth provided technical context explaining HTN as an AI planning technique from the 1970s used in game AI (F.E.A.R., Total War) and robotics, noting ElizaOS v2 added it for agent goal decomposition, though questioned the accuracy of this information. uplink2501 drew comparisons to Hierarchical Temporal Memory (HTM) and LISP. The conversation revealed uncertainty about HTN implementation details in ElizaOS v2 and its relationship to existing planning techniques.

faqs.question: What is HTN?
Is it true that ElizaOS v2 added HTN for agent goal decomposition?
Any similarity to Hierarchical Temporal Memory (HTM)? Or more like LISP?

helpInteractions.context: odilitime asked what HTN meant in the context of ElizaOS development
helpInteractions.resolution: thirti.eth explained HTN as Hierarchical Task Networks, an AI planning technique from the 1970s used in game AI and robotics, and its purported use in ElizaOS v2 for agent goal decomposition

actionItems.description: Upgrade HTN-lite to full HTN when Eliza v2 hits beta by replacing hand-coded decomposition with LLM planning
Verify and document whether ElizaOS v2 actually includes HTN for agent goal decomposition

categories.summary: The discussion centered on token utility strategy for Eliza, with shawmakesmagic explaining the challenge of implementing token-specific utility without creating user friction. The preferred approach is to accept any payment method users have and use revenue to buy back tokens. Key development updates include v3 nearing completion, an app in final stages, and Eliza Cloud actively taking business with direct app integration. A technical issue was reported regarding the Milady Play Store link from GitHub being non-functional. The team confirmed degenai updates are imminent. The conversation also included community banter about price movements and milestone-based commitments.

faqs.question: Does the Milady Play Store link from GitHub work?
Can we expect updates about degenai soon?

actionItems.description: Fix non-functional Milady Play Store link in GitHub repository
Complete and release v3 of Eliza
Finalize and launch the Eliza app with Eliza Cloud integration
Release degenai updates
